text: Overview

This report presents an integrative analysis of single-nucleus RNA-seq, GeoMx spatial transcriptomics, and bulk proteomics and phosphoproteomics across four mouse models of Alzheimer's disease. It asks how amyloid reshapes microglia under different tau backgrounds, reading the tau-amyloid interaction across all four modalities.

The synthesis comes first because the rebuilt answer is compact: amyloid drives a homeostatic-to-DAM microglial programme; mutant tau modulates that response mainly as extra DAM-cell composition rather than supported further trajectory progression; Myc has focused RNA support; NF-kB attenuation and Gsk3b are not recovered; SpatialDecon abundance is blocked and full CCC is not called. The remaining chapters are the audit trail. QC checks the modality shapes and the single-nucleus genotype-by-batch design, then the microglia, trajectory, mechanism and cross-modality chapters show the evidence behind each synthesis row.
The analysis is built on a reproducible spine: a pinned R environment (rv), a pinned Python environment (uv), a targets pipeline, and this single self-contained Quarto report.
Synthesis: the compact answer
The synthesis table has   claim rows:  . The supported core is narrow by design, and the negative rows are part of the result rather than a supplement.

What remains unsupported or unearned is also explicit:  . These limits keep the closed report focused on the rebuilt evidence: DAM activation and DAM-cell composition are the central interaction story; Myc is the focused RNA mechanism signal; NF-kB attenuation, Gsk3b, progression beyond composition, blocked SpatialDecon abundance, full CCC and run-order-sensitive bulk hits stay outside the supported claim set.
Quality control

This chapter is a sanity pass over the four loaded modalities. It confirms that each target materialises at the expected shape, that the single-nucleus microglia carry a fully-crossed genotype-by-batch design, and that their per-nucleus quality metrics sit inside predeclared physical bounds. No reprocessing happens here: clustering, substate assignment and differential expression arrive in later phases. The bounds below are definitional rails (a count is at least one, a percentage lies in nought to a hundred), not analysis thresholds, so a breach signals a load or computation fault and stops the render.

Sanity bounds (enforced)

The metric rails are definitional or physical, declared before inspecting the data: a count is at least one (no arbitrary upper ceiling); a detected-gene count is at least one and cannot exceed the genes assayed, nor the total counts; a percentage lies in nought to a hundred. The structural checks confirm the design grid, the canonical genotype factor and a one-to-one match between the 16 replicate ids and the genotype-by-batch cells. Any failure aborts the render.

section: Microglia: amyloid drives a homeostatic-to-DAM programme
text: Microglia: amyloid drives a homeostatic-to-DAM programme

section: Microglia: amyloid drives a homeostatic-to-DAM programme
text: This chapter is the single-nucleus core of the study. From the reprocessed, batch-integrated microglia (SCT normalisation, Harmony over batch only) it places each nucleus into a canonical substate, tests whether substate composition shifts across the genotype contrasts, and runs pseudobulk differential expression to characterise the activation programme and to weigh the tau-by-amyloid interaction. The headline, supported three complementary ways below, is that amyloid (NLGF) drives microglia out of a homeostatic state into disease-associated microglia (DAM); mutant tau scales the size of that response without, on its own, redrawing the programme. One measurement caveat frames everything that follows: single-nucleus capture under-detects a sizeable share of DAM activation transcripts (Thrupp and colleagues, 2020), so DAM is read as a scored programme rather than thresholded gene by gene, and every differential test runs on raw counts.
Substate landscape

UCell scores each nucleus against the canonical homeostatic, DAM, interferon-response (IFN) and proliferative signatures; a per-signature calibration then assigns a primary substate at the cluster level (the per-cell argmax is noisier and kept only as a diagnostic). After dropping   contaminant or doublet nuclei (clusters  ),   nuclei remain. They resolve into   homeostatic,   DAM and   IFN nuclei; no cluster is proliferative-dominant, so that level carries no cells (sparse cycling markers average out at the cluster level rather than forming a coherent population). Each substate signature is scored on its genes found in the assay (  genes used).

label: fig-microglia-umap
text: Microglia substate landscape on the batch-integrated UMAP. Left: primary substate label. Right: the calibrated DAM activation score, which forms a continuous homeostatic-to-DAM gradient rather than a discrete switch.

label: fig-microglia-umap-substate
text: Genotype-faceted substate map on the same batch-integrated UMAP. Amyloid-bearing genotypes occupy much more of the DAM-labelled region, with NLGF_P301S showing the largest DAM compartment. Labels are cluster-primary substates, not a per-cell hard threshold.

label: fig-microglia-score-triptych
text: Binned UMAP score maps for the three plotted signatures. Homeostatic and DAM scores form opposing regions of the activation manifold, while the MHC-APC score marks the antigen-presentation arm within the broader DAM programme.
Amyloid expands the DAM compartment

The compositional shift is unambiguous. Without amyloid the microglia are overwhelmingly homeostatic (DAM  % in MAPTKI,  % in P301S); with amyloid the DAM compartment swells to  % on the tau-knockout background and  % on the mutant-tau background, with the homeostatic fraction falling in step ( % and  % down to  % and  %). The same shift is visible per nucleus as a rightward move in the DAM activation score, and it is steeper on the mutant-tau background, the first hint of the tau-by-amyloid interaction tested below.

label: fig-microglia-composition-shift
text: Amyloid drives the homeostatic-to-DAM shift. Left: substate proportions per genotype (cluster-level labels). Right: per-nucleus DAM activation score by genotype; the amyloid-bearing genotypes carry the heavier upper tail.

label: fig-microglia-unit-composition
text: Composition by the 16 genotype-by-batch replicate units. Each bar is one replicate unit, preserving the inference unit behind the composition and pseudobulk tests. The DAM expansion is visible in every amyloid-bearing background rather than being driven by one batch.

label: fig-microglia-score-distribution
text: Score distributions by genotype within each primary substate. Amyloid shifts the DAM score upward and the homeostatic score downward while retaining overlap, consistent with a scored activation programme rather than a binary marker threshold.

section: Composition is tested across the genotype contrasts
text: Composition is tested across the genotype contrasts

section: Composition is tested across the genotype contrasts
text: Composition is tested formally with propeller (the locked, reproducible primary, on the logit transform) and cross-checked with sccomp, a Bayesian beta-binomial model run off-lock when its Stan backend is present. Both amyloid contrasts move DAM strongly up and the homeostatic fraction strongly down, with very small tail probabilities (table below). The tau-by-amyloid interaction on DAM is positive and significant under propeller (FDR  );  , so it is flagged rather than asserted. A static compositional interaction of this size raises a further question - does mutant tau also push microglia further along the same activation programme, or does it simply enlarge the DAM endpoint? The trajectory chapter ( ) takes that up directly and finds the latter: the synergy is carried by compartment size, not by cells advancing further along the axis.

label: fig-microglia-composition-forest

DAM and homeostatic move as mirror images; both amyloid contrasts are far from zero, the interaction is small.

The amyloid-to-DAM expression programme

Pseudobulk differential expression (limma-voom with quality weights, aggregated to the 16 genotype-by-batch replicate units) confirms the activation programme at the transcript level. Of   expressed genes,   pass the stageR family screen. The canonical DAM markers move up coherently under amyloid: of the   markers in the fit,  % rise on the tau-knockout background (mean log2 fold-change  ) and  % on the mutant-tau background (mean  ), reproducing the original study's direction. The volcano below shows the strongest amyloid contrast with the DAM markers picked out.

Amyloid contrasts carry thousands of FDR < 0.10 rows, whereas the interaction has real stageR-confirmed genes but no standalone large-effect FDR hits.

section: The tau-by-amyloid interaction is under-powered, not absent
text: interaction genes - every one with a substantial fold-change (all above the   log2 effect bar, median  ) that the stricter standalone per-contrast test cannot individually resolve (its most significant gene sits at FDR  ), so this is not an empty result. The difference-of-differences carries roughly twice the standard error of a main effect, and the pseudobulk design leaves few replicate degrees of freedom - eBayes moderation lifts the effective total to  : the minimum log2 fold-change detectable at a nominal 80% per-test power for the median gene is   (median posterior SE  ). The honest reading is therefore that a per-gene interaction below about   log2 units falls under this design's nominal detection floor - and the multiple-testing-corrected discovery floor is higher still - so absence of large-effect genes is not evidence of no interaction. Crucially, the compositional interaction on DAM *is* significant, which points to a synergy expressed through compartment size rather than per-gene fold-change. The trajectory chapter ( ) then separates the two ways that synergy could act - more DAM cells, or cells advancing further along the activation axis - and resolves it as the former: the tau-amyloid interaction adds DAM cells, with no statistically supported signal that it advances them further.
Per-substate differential expression

Repeating the pseudobulk fit within each substate is only valid where every replicate unit carries enough cells. Under a floor of   cells per unit, the substates that clear it on every unit ( ) are fitted; IFN (as few as   cells in a unit) and the empty proliferative level are left descriptive-only. Within the DAM compartment alone, amyloid still moves   genes past the large-effect bar (NLGF_P301S vs P301S), confirming the programme is graded - a shift in what DAM cells express, not merely how many cells are DAM.

label: fig-microglia-substate-audit
text: Per-substate DE fit audit. Bars show the minimum cells in any replicate unit, with the dashed line marking the pre-set floor for fitting. Homeostatic and DAM pass; IFN and the empty proliferative level remain descriptive.

label: fig-microglia-substate-volcano
text: Within-substate DE summary for the fitted homeostatic and DAM compartments. Bars are signed counts of FDR < 0.10 rows (up positive, down negative) for each contrast. Amyloid effects persist within states; the interaction remains much smaller than the amyloid main responses.
Caveats and provenance

Three caveats travel with these results. First, the Thrupp under-detection already noted: DAM is scored, not thresholded, and the differential tests run on raw counts, so the attenuation biases against the DAM signal rather than inventing it. Second, contaminant pruning removed proportionally more low-quality nuclei from the   background (about  % of it, mostly cluster  ) than from the others. Because every dropped cluster scores low on DAM - the highest dropped-cluster median ( ) falls well short of the DAM-enriched clusters that drive the headline (medians up to  , all retained) - the pruning cannot have manufactured the amyloid-to-DAM signal by discarding DAM-high nuclei. Removing low-DAM nuclei can only nudge a genotype's DAM fraction upward, so this genotype-skewed dropout is reported in full below rather than hidden. Third,

section: The tau-amyloid synergy adds DAM cells rather than advancing them
text: The tau-amyloid synergy adds DAM cells rather than advancing them

section: The tau-amyloid synergy adds DAM cells rather than advancing them
text: The microglia chapter left one question open. Amyloid drives the homeostatic-to-DAM shift, and the compositional interaction on DAM is significant: on a mutant-tau background amyloid produces *more* DAM cells than additivity predicts. But a larger DAM compartment and cells that have travelled *further* into activation are two different claims, and the pseudobulk interaction was under-powered to separate them. This chapter orders the same nuclei along an activation pseudotime and asks the distinct question the composition test cannot: beyond making more DAM cells, does mutant tau push microglia further along the activation axis? Read three ways below, it does not detectably - no channel gives statistically supported evidence of further advance, and the tau-amyloid synergy is expressed almost entirely as compartment size rather than as cells sitting further along the axis. Two framings travel with every number here: pseudotime is an activation *ordering* (position and extent of advance), not developmental time or potency, and because the data are a single cross-sectional snapshot, "advance" is a position statement, never a measured rate.

section: Amyloid drives a large shift along the activation axis
text: Amyloid drives a large shift along the activation axis

section: Amyloid drives a large shift along the activation axis
text: Slingshot orders the homeostatic and DAM nuclei along a single rooted lineage on the batch-integrated harmony space (the interferon and proliferative nuclei are off this axis and omitted, recovered as a conditioning check below). Rooting is validated after the fact, not assumed: pseudotime rises with the DAM score (Spearman  ) and falls with the homeostatic score ( ), so low pseudotime is the homeostatic end and high pseudotime the DAM end. Against that ordering the amyloid effect is unmistakable and large: it moves mean pseudotime up by   units on the tau-knockout background (NLGF_MAPTKI vs MAPTKI, p  ) and   units on the mutant-tau background (NLGF_P301S vs P301S, p  ). Tau on its own moves it little ( ). These amyloid main effects dwarf everything that follows; the interaction is a second-order adjustment on top of them.

Right: the interaction in context - the genotype effects on mean pseudotime across the five canonical contrasts (point and 95% CI); the two amyloid contrasts are large, the difference-of-differences interaction (crimson) is a modest positive adjustment.

section: The interaction is composition, not progression
text: The interaction is composition, not progression

section: The interaction is composition, not progression
text: The difference-of-differences interaction raises mean pseudotime position by   units (95% CI [ ,  ], ordinary p  , Freedman-Lane permutation p  ) - microglia on the amyloid-plus-mutant-tau background sit a little further along the axis than an additive model predicts. The exact three-channel shift-share decomposition (composition, progression, cross) says where that advance comes from, and the two channels point in opposite directions. The composition channel - the same per-state positions, with cells redistributed toward the DAM end - carries the whole effect (interaction  , p  ; loading   of the position interaction). The progression channel - cells of a given state sitting further along, the genuinely abundance-independent quantity - is small, negative and not significant ( , p  , permutation p  ; loading  ), and the cross term is minor (loading  ). The three loadings sum to one by construction (reconstruction residual  ).

section: The interaction is composition, not progression
label: fig-trajectory-decomposition
text: The position interaction is compositional. Left: the tau-amyloid interaction on each additive pseudotime channel (point and 95% CI, all in pseudotime units); the composition channel is positive and clears zero, while the progression and within-state channels do not. Right: the shift-share loadings - composition takes more than the whole position interaction, progression is negative, and they sum to one. Mutant tau enlarges the DAM compartment; it does not measurably advance cells within a state.

section: The interaction is composition, not progression
text: The pre-registered primary endpoints - the progression channel and the within-homeostatic mean position, both designed to be insensitive to compartment size - are both non-significant (BH-adjusted FDR   and  ). So the honest reading is that there is no statistically supported signal that mutant tau advances microglia further along the activation axis; what the interaction does, it does by enlarging the DAM compartment. That is the trajectory-level counterpart of P1's significant DAM-fraction interaction: the same synergy, now resolved as compositional. The full inference across every endpoint, including the bounded fraction-past-onset bridges, is tabulated below.

section: The interaction is composition, not progression
label: fig-trajectory-unit-pt-dam
text: Replicate-level mean pseudotime tracks the DAM fraction. Each point is one genotype-by-batch unit; batch labels show that the high-position units are also high-DAM units. This is why the decomposition is load-bearing: a position interaction can be created by changing compartment size.

section: The interaction is composition, not progression
label: fig-trajectory-kitagawa-forest
text: Channel audit for the trajectory inference. Left: prepared overall and within-state rows across the five contrasts; amyloid moves mean position, but the interaction's within-state rows do not clear zero. Right: the explicit interaction decomposition rows; composition is positive, progression is negative and not supported.

section: A per-cell model corroborates the position shift
text: A per-cell model corroborates the position shift

section: A per-cell model corroborates the position shift
text: The summary-level test aggregates each of the   genotype-by-batch units to one number, which is the right scale for the interaction inference but discards the per-cell distribution. As a supportive cross-check, a per-cell beta generalised linear mixed model (pt01 ~ tau * amyloid + batch + (1 | unit),   on-lineage nuclei over   replicate units)  . This agrees that the interaction raises position. But it models per-cell position directly, so it is the per-cell analogue of mean pseudotime and is itself composition-conflated: it supports that position rises, not that progression does. It is reported as supportive only - the summary-level decomposition carries the composition-versus-progression inference, and there both concordance and discordance with this arm would be acceptable.
Reconciliation, robustness and concordance

This re-baselines a v1 result rather than reproducing it. v1's point estimate had loaded the same interaction mostly onto progression (about   of the effect) at a suggestive significance (FDR about  ); on R   the decomposition attributes it mainly to composition instead, and neither the progression nor the within-state channel clears significance. The qualitative headline - a real, modest tau-amyloid synergy carried by the DAM compartment - is unchanged; its mechanistic reading shifts from "further-progressed cells" toward "more cells". The ordering itself is stable and marker-concordant: refitting on 10 and 20 harmony dimensions and on all retained nuclei reproduces the primary pseudotime at Spearman   or better, and the slingshot ordering agrees with the marker score-axis at Spearman   (a moderate-to-large concordance, expected below one because the transcriptome geometry and the DAM-minus-homeostatic marker contrast are related, not identical).

label: fig-trajectory-concordance
text: The slingshot pseudotime and the assumption-light marker score-axis (DAM minus homeostatic UCell) order the on-lineage nuclei concordantly; the binned density runs along the diagonal. This shares the marker system that defines the substates, so it guards against gross ordering failure rather than acting as an independent check.

label: fig-trajectory-audit
text: Trajectory robustness and lineage-conditioning audit. Left: refits using fewer or more harmony dimensions, and all retained nuclei, remain strongly concordant with the primary pseudotime. Right: the off-lineage omitted fraction is low within every genotype, so conditioning on the homeostatic-to-DAM lineage does not drive the interaction.
Caveats and provenance

Five caveats frame this chapter. First, pseudotime is an activation ordering, not developmental time or cellular potency: direction rests on slingshot rooting and the validated DAM-marker monotonicity above, never on a potency proxy. Second, and for the same reason, every effect here is a statement about position and extent of advance in a cross-sectional snapshot; the rate or acceleration reading would need longitudinal sampling or RNA velocity, which this design does not have. Third, the lineage conditions on the homeostatic-to-DAM cells, omitting the interferon and proliferative nuclei ( % of microglia overall); were that omission genotype-skewed it could bias the interaction, so the per-genotype omitted fraction is reported below and is balanced across genotypes. Fourth, the within-state and composition channels are anchored on cell-weighted pooled positions, so the decomposition is conditional on that anchoring. Fifth, the substates are transcriptionally close, which is why the marker score-axis concordance is moderate rather than near-perfect and is read as a guard, not an independent validation.

section: Mechanism: Myc signal, NF-kB check, and bulk kinase support
text: Mechanism: Myc signal, NF-kB check, and bulk kinase support

section: Mechanism: Myc signal, NF-kB check, and bulk kinase support
text: P3 reframes the mechanism question around the rebuilt P1/P2 result. Amyloid drives a microglial homeostatic-to-DAM programme, and mutant tau modulates that amyloid response mainly by expanding the DAM compartment, not by a supported further advance along the activation ordering. The analyses below ask which RNA pathway, TF and kinase signals plausibly sit under that state expansion. They are treated as tests, not as assumed winners: Myc and Gsk3b can be supported or not by the rebuilt data, and NF-kB attenuation is only called if its predeclared transcript-level gate clears.
Pathway Survey

The project gene sets recapitulate the upstream story in RNA. The whole-microglia DAM set is positive in the tau-amyloid interaction (NES  , FDR  ), aligning with the P1 DAM-composition interaction (DAM propeller ratio  , FDR  ) and the P2 composition channel (effect  , FDR  ). The progression channel remains non-significant (effect  , FDR  ), so the mechanism target is DAM-cell expansion and amyloid-response modulation, not proven further activation of cells already on the trajectory.

label: fig-mechanism-project-sets
text: Focused project gene sets in whole microglia. Tile colour is fgsea NES over the pseudobulk t-statistic ranks; outlined points mark FDR < 0.10 within the project collection and contrast. The DAM set stays positive in the interaction, while the NF-kB target arms are read through the formal gate below.

label: fig-mechanism-project-pathway
text: Project gene-set survey across populations. Tile colour is fgsea NES; outlined points mark FDR < 0.10 within population and contrast. The interaction pattern is read against the closed P1/P2 result: DAM-cell expansion, not supported progression beyond composition.

label: fig-mechanism-go-dotplot
text: Compact GO survey from the bounded fgsea layer. Each population-contrast panel contributes its top two GO rows; colour is NES, size is gene-set size, and filled points mark FDR < 0.10. These rows are exploratory context, not a new mechanism call.
TF Activity

The strongest TF result is Myc repression in the interaction. In whole microglia, Myc has ULM score   (FDR  ); in the DAM substate it is also negative (score  , FDR  ). This is the cleanest rebuilt support for the v1 Myc mechanism thread. It should still be read as a regulatory-activity estimate from static pseudobulk RNA ranks, not as a perturbation experiment.

label: fig-mechanism-tf-interaction
text: Top TF activities for the tau-amyloid interaction. Scores are decoupleR ULM activities over CollecTRI, FDR-adjusted within population and contrast. Myc is highlighted because it is a predeclared v1 mechanism candidate and is the strongest rebuilt TF signal.

label: fig-mechanism-tf-lollipop
text: Focused TF candidates across the interaction and tau-in-NLGF contrasts. Myc is consistently negative and supported, while NF-kB-family members do not form a concordant negative attenuation pattern. Filled points mark FDR < 0.10 within population and contrast.
NF-kB Attenuation
Supportive rows, including tau-in-NLGF and substates, remain descriptive and cannot override the whole-microglia interaction gate.

label: fig-mechanism-nfkb-discordance
text: NF-kB attenuation verdict from the guarded mechanism target. The two whole-microglia interaction rows are primary, and the plot title prints the current target status. Tau-in-NLGF rows are supportive-only and cannot override the primary gate.
Gsk3b And Kinase Activity

The KSN layer has adequate coverage for the specific candidate:   Gsk3b substrate sites pass the min-size rule, from   matched KSN sites and   kinases passing min-size overall. But the rebuilt Gsk3b interaction score is   (FDR  ) and the tau-in-NLGF score is   (FDR  ), so the v1 Gsk3b interaction headline is not recovered here.

The kinase rows that do pass the primary FDR threshold are concentrated in tau-in-NLGF (  non-Gsk3b kinases at FDR <  ). None are supported by the additive run-index sensitivity (  retain FDR support with concordant sign), so the bulk phosphosite layer is hypothesis-generating. It is 24-month bulk hippocampus phosphoproteomics, not microglia-sorted, and its 16 runs are genotype-blocked, so it should not be used as a stand-alone microglial mechanism claim.

label: fig-mechanism-kinase-heatmap
text: Kinase activity rows from the 24-month bulk hippocampus phosphosite layer. Gsk3b is carried in every contrast whether significant or not; other kinases appear because they pass primary FDR < 0.10. White circles mark primary support and crosses mark additive run-index support when present.
Synthesis

The rebuilt mechanism evidence is asymmetric. The RNA layer strongly supports a Myc-linked, DAM-centred interaction signal, and the pathway layer ties that signal back to the compositional state expansion established in P1/P2. The targeted NF-kB attenuation check is discordant rather than supported, and the 24-month bulk phosphosite kinase layer does not recover a significant Gsk3b interaction. P3 therefore strengthens the DAM/Myc mechanism hypothesis while downgrading Gsk3b and NF-kB attenuation to unresolved or unsupported within the rebuilt data. The closing synthesis treats those negatives as part of the result, while the cross-modality chapter audits whether spatial, total-proteome and broader phosphoproteome views add corroborating context.

section: Cross-modality: spatial, bulk, and divergence checks
text: Cross-modality: spatial, bulk, and divergence checks

section: Cross-modality: spatial, bulk, and divergence checks
text: P4 asks what the non-snRNAseq modalities add to the rebuilt microglia story. The spatial and bulk layers are not treated as independent winners: GeoMx AOIs are repeated tissue observations, and the 24-month proteome and phosphoproteome are bulk hippocampus, not microglia-sorted. The chapter therefore reads them as concordance, caveat and divergence checks around the P1-P3 spine: amyloid-to-DAM activation, a DAM-composition tau-amyloid interaction, Myc-linked RNA support, and unsupported NF-kB/Gsk3b mechanism claims.
GeoMx Spatial DE

The GeoMx layer contributes a tissue-spatial transcriptomic readout over   AOIs and   biological units. Its primary model uses slide fixed effects plus duplicate-correlation blocking by biological unit, so the AOIs are not read as independent animals. Amyloid produces large spatial DE counts in both backgrounds (  rows at FDR <   on MAPTKI,   on P301S), while the tau-amyloid interaction is much smaller (  rows). That fits the broader story: GeoMx strongly corroborates the amyloid tissue response, but interaction claims still lean on the microglia composition and trajectory layers.

label: fig-crossmodality-geomx-counts
text: GeoMx spatial DE counts under the primary AOI model with bio-unit blocking. Bars count primary GeoMx rows with FDR < 0.10, split by logFC direction. The amyloid contrasts dominate; the interaction has far fewer spatial transcriptomic hits.

label: fig-crossmodality-geomx-volcano
text: GeoMx primary-model volcanoes across the P4 focus contrasts. Points are pre-binned from the AOI model with bio-unit blocking, and labels mark the strongest rows per contrast. The amyloid contrasts have dense high-significance structure; the interaction is visibly smaller.

label: fig-crossmodality-geomx-sensitivity
text: GeoMx bio-unit-blocked primary calls against the two sensitivity fits. Positive bars are rows gained by a sensitivity fit, negative bars are primary rows lost. The collapsed biological-unit fit loses many primary rows, so the chapter privileges the duplicate-correlation primary model rather than reading AOIs as independent animals.
Bulk Proteome And Phospho

The bulk layers use the same 16-run, 24-month hippocampus sample set: total protein, raw phosphosite and parent-protein-corrected phosphosite. The feature universes are compact enough to report directly ( ). The run-index sensitivity is load-bearing. Across bulk layers,   primary FDR <   rows are checked, and   ( %) lose support or flip under the additive run-index sensitivity. The most affected cell is   in   (  rows), so bulk signals below are treated as support only when they survive that caveat.

label: fig-crossmodality-bulk-counts
text: Bulk omics feature counts at FDR < 0.10. Proteome, raw phosphosite and parent-protein-corrected phosphosite layers are 24-month bulk hippocampus, not microglia-sorted. Many primary hits are sensitive to genotype-blocked run order.

label: fig-crossmodality-bulk-run-index
text: Run-index sensitivity across the 24-month bulk hippocampus layers. Tile fill is the fraction of primary FDR < 0.10 rows that lose support or flip sign after additive run-index adjustment; text is lost or flipped over primary hits. Most bulk signal is run-order-sensitive, so it is corroborative rather than a stand-alone microglial mechanism claim.

label: fig-crossmodality-phospho-correction
text: Raw versus parent-protein-corrected phosphosite effects in the 24-month bulk hippocampus layer. Points are pre-binned by site-level log2 fold-change, with selected top sites labelled. Parent correction changes effect geometry but does not turn this bulk phospho layer into a microglia-sorted mechanism assay.

label: fig-crossmodality-anchor-heatmap
text: Bulk anchor coverage and effects for clearance, synaptic, complement and Gsk3b-related rows. Each tile is the best available row per anchor symbol, layer and contrast, selected by FDR then absolute t-statistic; outlined points mark FDR < 0.10. Blank cells are unmeasured or filtered in that bulk layer.

Integrated Divergence

The integration target keeps nulls and mixed signs rather than compressing them into a v1-style claims ledger. Across the four focus contrasts it carries   symbol-contrast rows observed in at least two broad modality classes,   rows with any significant modality, and   mixed-sign rows. Mixed signs are expected in this design because microglia RNA, spatial tissue RNA, total protein and phosphosite statistics answer related but non-identical questions.

P4 Synthesis

Cross-modality evidence strengthens the amyloid-response spine. GeoMx and bulk layers both show their largest effects in amyloid contrasts, pathway rows reproduce antigen-presentation, phagocytic/clearance and synaptic axes, and  . The same evidence narrows the claims: the tau-amyloid interaction is not broadly spatial or bulk-omics significant, SpatialDecon abundance is blocked after fitting, full CCC is not called, and genotype-blocked bulk run order downgrades many proteome/phosphosite rows. In the closing synthesis, this chapter therefore corroborates  , while keeping progression, NF-kB attenuation, Gsk3b and spatial abundance unresolved or unsupported in the rebuilt data.
